scenario: You are working with an established model-driven app that allows users to log upcoming travel requests and bookings. Users have reported a script error on opening the Contact form, which you have replicated. Using the Monitor tool, you examined the FormEvents.onload operation. The onload event handlers are: eventIndex 0 = Mscrm.Form_onload (library Account/Account_main_system_library.js), eventIndex 1 = enableBPFfields (library travel_AccountJS), eventIndex 2 = OnLoad (library travel_AccountJS); the onchange for statecode handler is validateAreaCode (library travel_AccountJS). AllLibraries: index 0 = travel_AccountJS, index 1 = travel_StateAreaCodeOptions, index 2 = $webresource:Account/Account_main_system_library.js. You perform the following two edits to the Account record URL and the error still occurs: (1) ...&flags=DisableFormHandlers=true_0_2  (2) ...&flags=DisableFormLibraries=1_2. You need to identify the cause of the error.
q: Which library or function should you identify?
why: DisableFormHandlers=true_0_2 disabled onload handlers at index 0 (Mscrm.Form_onload) and index 2 (OnLoad), and DisableFormLibraries=1_2 disabled libraries at index 1 (travel_StateAreaCodeOptions) and index 2 (Account_main_system_library.js). The error still occurred, so the culprit must be something still enabled: onload handler index 1 (enableBPFfields) which comes from the travel_AccountJS library (AllLibraries index 0, which was NOT disabled). Therefore the travel_AccountJS library is the cause. The specific functions/other libraries were already disabled by the flags, so they cannot be the cause.
